title: Rotate Image
description: Given an n x n 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise). You need to do this in place, which means you have to modify the input matrix directly without using any additional space. To achieve this, you can follow these steps: first, transpose the matrix and then reverse each row.

examples.explanation: After transposing the matrix, it becomes [[1, 4, 7], [2, 5, 8], [3, 6, 9]]. Reversing each row results in [[7, 4, 1], [8, 5, 2], [9, 6, 3]].
After transposing, the matrix becomes [[ 5,  2, 13, 15], [ 1,  4,  3, 14], [ 9,  8,  6, 12], [11, 10,  7, 16]]. Reversing each row gives us the final rotated matrix.
Transposing the matrix results in [[1, 3], [2, 4]]. After reversing each row, we get [[3, 1], [4, 2]].

constraints: The input matrix will always be a square matrix (n x n).
hints: Think about how you can achieve rotation using transposition followed by reversal.
Consider how the elements shift their positions during rotation.
Pay attention to the indices of the elements while transposing.
Try visualizing the matrix on paper to better understand the transformation.
